summary: Current-head unit/integration coverage is sufficient: concrete fixtures exercise the full guarded Run state, authority, migration, lock, recovery, CLI, and legacy-compatibility surfaces, while inherited responsibility regressions cover the adjacent PR lifecycle, runtime-cost, and managed-worktree contracts.
inspection_summary: At HEAD e0a38eb3c62aea492bdfc0c6227e3a39734dc7c2 I compared the Story/Architecture test contract and path-surface matrix against the implementation and all 45 focused test cases, inspected the current-bound unit/integration/JUnit evidence plus the qualified full-suite delta, and independently reran test/guarded-run-session.test.js (45 passed, 0 failed).
inspection_inputs: git diff main...HEAD -- src/cli.js src/git-identity.js src/guarded-run-session.js test/guarded-run-session.test.js
judgment_delta: Initial concern: a 1,144-line persistence module and central execute CLI routing could be protected mainly by happy-path tests -> Final conclusion: the 45-test fixture suite directly asserts pre-fix-sensitive state bytes, transition counts, typed errors, mutation absence, authority/mirror ordering, restart behavior, invalid candidates, lock contention, and CLI output; the independent current-head rerun passed 45/45.

Initial concern: additive execute routes might regress legacy PR lifecycle and managed-worktree behavior -> Final conclusion: the legacy status route is asserted byte-compatible and nonmutating in the focused suite, while current-head inherited-responsibility JUnits pass 4 PR-lifecycle, 22 runtime-cost, and 12 managed-worktree tests (38/38) without overclaiming ownership of those surfaces.

Initial concern: the repository-wide 1,138-test run was not a single-head execution -> Final conclusion: the evidence explicitly qualifies that limitation; the complete 6cb1101..e0a38eb delta is only src/cli.js, src/guarded-run-session.js, and test/guarded-run-session.test.js, and those changed surfaces are covered by exact-head unit/E2E evidence plus this independent 45/45 rerun.

Initial concern: fallback, migration, repair, rejected-selection, human-rendering, and generated evidence surfaces might be silent or compositional gaps -> Final conclusion: named fixtures cover each surface with observable warnings/errors or byte-level assertions, and the test plan accurately labels compositional rather than Cartesian coverage; no unverified critical path was found within this Story's persistence-only boundary.
